page: Microsoft Azure E4as_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Eav6 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 3.7 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Cache specifications include 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU scoring 3066.27 and Geekbench single-core reaching 2102.0, both placing in the top 25% of tested instances. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench text processing also demonstrate strong performance. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics show some limitations. Multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores fall into the average category. Memory read bandwidth is weak, measuring 303.52 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 37.9 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, though large block write bandwidth is average at 28.01 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations are weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

Given its hardware profile and benchmark results, the E4as_v6 is suited for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high-throughput local storage or massive parallel processing. Appropriate workloads include memory-cached applications, moderate web serving, and lightweight text or image processing. It is less suited for heavy database operations, software compilation, or medium-to-large language model inference, where prompt processing for a 7B model drops to a poor 11.74 tokens/sec. The instance offers a cost-efficient entry point for memory-heavy tasks that benefit from dedicated AMD EPYC execution without the overhead of local NVMe drives.
description: The Microsoft Azure E4as_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the Eav6 family, featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical AMD EPYC 9V74 cores running at 3.7 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio, but lacks local storage and integrated GPUs. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2102.0 and strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core performance and memory read bandwidth are average to weak, with Redis SET operations and database benchmarks falling into the bottom 25%. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive workloads such as caching layers, moderate web serving, and light text processing, while being less optimal for heavy database operations or multi-threaded software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4as_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized VM featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance, making it suitable for memory-intensive caching and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E4as_v6 is a memory-optimized VM with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 32 GB RAM, designed for memory-intensive workloads without local storage.
tagline: A dedicated AMD-powered virtual machine offering high memory-to-core density and strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 32 GB of RAM, offering a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
No local storage included, but compatible with Premium Storage
Best suited for memory-cached applications, light text processing, and web serving
